hero_subtitle: SFTY helps regulatory teams link suspicious transactions with VASP data, case evidence, and supervisory reporting in a secure, audit-ready investigation platform.
trust_card_1_value: Market participants, licensing data and risk profiles
trust_card_2_value: Wallets, transactions and exposure across the market
trust_card_3_value: Originator and beneficiary data checked for completeness
trust_card_5_value: On-premise or private cloud for sensitive data
trust_card_6_value: Supervisory outputs, evidence and regulatory reports
problem_description: FIU teams struggle to connect fragmented signals, VASP data and ownership context into a coherent case workflow. This disconnection slows suspicious transaction reviews and weakens the ability to produce defensible evidence for regulatory reporting.
problem_point_1_value: VASP activity, licensing data and risk signals remain scattered
problem_point_2_value: Alerts and investigations depend on disconnected manual processes
problem_point_3_value: Decisions are harder to explain without connected logs and case history
problem_point_4_value: Regulatory teams lack one shared operating layer for supervision
solution_description: SFTY links fragmented alerts, VASP profiles, ownership data and investigative workflows into one structured environment for FIUs to efficiently review suspicious transactions and prepare audit-ready reporting evidence.
workflow_title: From suspicious transaction signals to reports
workflow_description: SFTY moves suspicious activity alerts through VASP context, case review, evidence collection and supervisory reporting within a structured FIU investigation workflow.
workflow_step_1_description: Wallet, VASP, Travel Rule or OSINT signal appears
workflow_step_2_description: SFTY classifies and routes the signal for review
workflow_step_3_description: A supervisory case opens with context & evidence
workflow_step_4_description: Teams record a decision, request or supervisory response
workflow_step_5_description: Evidence, actions and outcomes stay available for review
why_sfty_title: Not another analytics tool. One evidence-ready layer
why_sfty_description: SFTY gives FIUs a secure supervisory layer that connects suspicious transaction signals, VASP context, cases, evidence and reporting for structured AML intelligence workflows.
why_sfty_card_1_description: See activity across VASPs, wallets, entities and risk signals
why_sfty_card_2_description: Connect participants, structures, links and supervisory records
why_sfty_card_3_description: Move from alerts to investigations, actions and reporting
why_sfty_card_4_description: Understand not only single risks, but the condition of the market
authority_description: SFTY enables regulators to manage suspicious transaction reporting with secure deployment, controlled access and evidence-grade logging in a procurement-safe government environment.
authority_card_1_description: Deploy on-premise, in private cloud or inside a controlled government environment
authority_card_2_description: Preserve activity, decisions, case history and reporting evidence
authority_card_3_description: Control who can view, review, escalate and act across teams and agencies
authority_card_4_description: Support phased implementation, documentation, integrations and governance review
ask_sfty_placeholder: Ask SFTY how suspicious transaction signals connect to case evidence
ask_sfty_chip_1_message: Explain how FIU teams structure suspicious activity cases
ask_sfty_chip_2_message: Show how evidence-grade logging supports audit-ready reporting
ask_sfty_chip_3_message: Describe how SFTY supports multi-provider data orchestration
ask_sfty_helper_text: AI assistant for supervisory, deployment and compliance questions
faq_description: Clear answers on VASP supervision, sovereign deployment, evidence logging and regulatory workflows.
faq_1_question: What is SFTY for AML authorities?
faq_1_answer: SFTY provides AML authorities a secure supervisory layer connecting VASP data, transaction activity, risk alerts, cases and reports for structured suspicious transaction oversight.
faq_2_question: How does SFTY handle suspicious transaction cases?
faq_2_answer: SFTY structures suspicious signals into cases with linked entity, wallet, transaction and risk context, supporting review notes, decisions, escalation and reporting workflows.
faq_3_question: How does SFTY preserve supervisory evidence?
faq_3_answer: SFTY preserves case history, decision logs and supporting evidence with audit-grade records to support regulatory review and supervisory accountability.
faq_4_question: Can SFTY integrate with existing provider data?
faq_4_answer: Yes. SFTY connects outputs from multiple providers, normalizes data and orchestrates workflows without replacing existing analytics or compliance systems.
faq_5_question: Does SFTY replace existing supervisory tools?
faq_5_answer: No. SFTY operates above existing supervisory tools, linking fragmented data sources into one evidence-ready, auditable government compliance environment.
final_title: Structure suspicious transaction workflows with SFTY
final_description: Review how SFTY connects suspicious signals, VASP context, case evidence and reporting for FIU and AML supervisory workflows.
footer_description: Supervisory and compliance control layer for digital asset markets, businesses and regulated institutions.
footer_contact_title: For supervisory, compliance and deployment discussions.
footer_legal_notice: This page is for informational purposes only and does not constitute legal, regulatory or compliance advice. SFTY deployment depends on jurisdiction, client requirements and applicable laws.
